explanation: This command uses the /i flag with an HTTP URL starting with an IP address (127.0.0.1), which matches the Sigma rule's detection pattern for regsvr32 downloading from an IP-based URL.
This command uses the -i flag instead of /i and omits the colon after the flag, but still specifies an HTTP URL with an IP address. This is a valid variation that matches the Sigma rule.
This command uses a backslash in the URL path instead of a forward slash, which is a valid mutation technique. It still matches the Sigma rule's pattern for an IP-based URL.
This command uses HTTPS instead of HTTP and includes a valid IP address in the URL. It matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command adds quotes around the DLL filename, which is a safe mutation technique. It still matches the Sigma rule's pattern for an IP-based URL.
